name: Espresso-Hazelnut Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Hazelnuts, dry-roasted and coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Bon Appetit Magazine
For crust:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Generously butter the bottom and sides of a 9-inch diameter springform pan with 2-3/4-inch high sides. Wrap the outside of the pan with a double layer of foil.
Finely grind cookies, hazelnuts, sugar, and cinnamon in a food processor. Add melted butter; process until moist clumps form. Press crumb mixture onto the bottom and up the sides of the pan. Chill while preparing filling.
For filling:
Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese in a large bowl until smooth. Add sugar and beat until well blended. Add eggs one at a time, beating just until blended and scraping down the sides of the bowl after each addition. Beat in sour cream and 1/2 cup whipping cream. Stir espresso powder and 2 tablespoons warm water in a small bowl until powder dissolves. Add to filling and beat until blended. Beat in vanilla. Stir in chopped hazelnuts.
Pour filling into prepared pan. Place pan in a large baking pan. Pour enough hot water into the baking pan to come halfway up the sides of the springform pan.
Bake cake until top is puffed and center is almost set, about 1-1/4 hours. Turn off oven; open door slightly. Let cake stand in oven with door ajar for 1 hour. Remove pan from water and transfer to rack. Cool. Wrap in foil and chill overnight (can be made up to 3 days in advance).
Run a small knife around the sides of the pan to loosen the cheesecake. Remove the pan sides.
Beat 2/3 cup whipping cream until stiff peaks form using a large star-tipped bag. Pipe around the top edge. Decorate with Espresso Beans.
